title: The One Where Joey Speaks French

lines.speaker: Produced by
lines.text: Robert Carlock & Wendy Knoller
Central Perk. Everyone's sitting on the couch and Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey! Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How was the honeymoon?
Phoebe; Oh, incredible! Oh! Champagne, candle-lit dinners, moonlight walks on the beach, it was sooo ro-man-tic!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, where's Mike?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, he's at the doctor, he didn't poop the whole time we were there!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well anyway, I'm glad you're back, I really need your help.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, why? What's up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I have an audition for this play and for some of it I have to speak French. Which, according to my resume, I'm fluent in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey, you shouldn't lie on your resume.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, you really shouldn't. (to Ross, sarcastically) By the way, how was that year-long dig in Cairo?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (whispering) It was ok...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I did not know you spoke French.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oui, bien sur je parle Francais! Qu'est-ce que tu penses alors?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh... you're so sexy!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, so, will you help me? I really wanna be in this play.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure! Tout le plaisir est pour moi, mon ami.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Seriously stop it, or I'm gonna jump on ya.
OPENING CREDITS
Monica and Chandler's apartment.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why are you wearing my apron?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm making cookies for Erica. And oh, by the way, we have to leave for the airport soon, her plane comes in about an hour.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, hey, when she gets here, is it ok if I introduce you two as "my wife" and "the woman who's carrying my child"? (she's not amused) No? Divorce?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (he enters) Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You guys know where Rachel is?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, we haven't seen her since this morning.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So unbelievable. She was supposed to meet me half an hour ago with Emma. (he tries to take a cookie but Monica slaps his hand)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: These are for Erica!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? She's gonna eat all those cookies?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I want he baby to come out all cute and fat!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, why is Erica coming to visit?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, because we want to get to know her better and she's never been to New York so she wants to see all the tourists' spots... you know, Statue of Liberty, Empire State Building...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, those places! There's always so many people, their being corralled like cattle, and... you know, there's always some idiot who goes "Mooooo"!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, if it annoys you so much, then why do you do it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looking at Rachel entering with Emma) Oh, hi! Hi! Thanks for showing you up thirty minutes late!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, no, I'm sure you have a great excuse, wh-was it a hair appointment, a mani-pedi or was there a sale at Barney's?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: My father had an heart attack... (crying) ...while I was at Barney's.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm so sorry...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Is-is he ok?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, they said he's gonna be fine, but he's still heavily sedated.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, ok. I'm gonna come out to Long Island with you, I mean, you can't be alone right now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, come on, I'm totally ok. (hugging him) I don't need you to come! I can totally handle this on my own.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Still-still, let me come... for me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok. If you really need to.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I bet someone could use one of Monica's freshly baked cookies.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I really could.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh... (Ross mouths HA-HA at Monica and takes two cookies and she looks at him angrily)
Central Perk. Phoebe's trying to teach Joey French, so she's sitting in front of him with the script in her hands.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, it seems pretty simple. Your first line is "My name is Claude", so, just repeat after me. "Je m'appelle Claude".

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Je de coup Clow.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, just... let's try it again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Je m'appelle Claude.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Je depli mblue.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh. It's not... quite what I'm saying.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really? It sounds exactly the same to me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It does, really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, let just try it again. Really listen.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Got it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (slowly) Je m'appelle Claude.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Je te flouppe Fli.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, mon Dieu!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, de fuff!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering with Erica and Chandler) Hey you guys.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I want you to meet someone really special. Phoebe, this is Erica. And this is the baby!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey. Erica, baby!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Everyone. Erica, baby!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica. Calm, self.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Thank you. It's really nice to meet you guys, I can't believe I'm here!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Welcome to New York City! Or should I say "ghe deu flooff New York City"?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why would you say that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok. What are you gonna be doing today?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: I wanna see everything! Times Square, Coney Island, Rockefeller Center...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, you know what you should do? You should walk all the way at the top of Statue of Liberty.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Oh yeah, let's do that!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Great! (to Monica) This baby'd better to be really good.
Hospital.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (stopping a nurse who's coming out of a room) Oh, uhm, excuse me, I'm here to see my father. My name is Rachel Green.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And I'm Doctor Ross Geller.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, please, this is a hospital, ok? That actually means something here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can somebody please go in?

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, I think I'm gonna wait out here, because my throat is feeling a little scratchy, I don't want to infect him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, please, don't be so scared of him!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm not scared of him, I'm really sick!

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: He's under sedation, so he's pretty much out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm feeling better.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! (They enter. Rachel sees his father, lying on a bed, with tubes, drip and everything) Oh! Oh my God! Ohhh, ohhh, wow, that ear and nose hair trimmer I got him was just money down the drain, huh?

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Miss Green, your father's doctor is on the phone if you'd like to speak to him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, great, Are you gonna be ok?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He's unconscious, I think we'll be just fine!
Rachel (leaving): Ok.

Ross goes into the room where Dr. Green is laying unconscious. He turns on the TV, puts his feet on the bed and starts watching a dinosaur movie where the dinosaur is caught by two cowboys. Dr. Geller awakes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did the TV wake you?

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: No, when you put your feet up in my bed, you tugged on my catheter.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ouchy.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: What are you doing here, Geller?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I came with Rachel, who should be back any second! (pause) So what's new?

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Ooh, I have a little heart attack.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right, is it painful?

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: What, the heart attack or sitting here talking to you?
Ross (he buzzes for the nurse) Let's see if we can get that Rachel back here.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: So what's new with you, uh, knocked up any more of my daughters lately?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nope, just the one. RACH!
Joey's apartment. Phoebe is trying to teach Joey French.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Je m'appelle Claude.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Je do call blue!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Noooo! Ok, maybe if we just break it down. Ok, let's try at one syllable at a time. Ok? So repeat after me. "je".

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: je.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: m'ap

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: mah

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: pelle

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: pel.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great, ok faster! "je"

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: je.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: m'ap

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: mah

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: pelle

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: pel.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Je m'appelle!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me pooh pooh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, it's too hard, I can't teach you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I, I have to go before I put your head through a wall. (she leaves)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (he goes out calling her) Don't move! Don't go! I need you! My audition is tomorrow! Shah blue blah! Me lah peeh! Ombrah! (he gives up). Pooh.
Green's mansion. Rachel's Room. Rachel's is combing her hair; Ross's coming into the room

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I was gonna make us some dinner but all I found in your dad's fridge was bacon and heavy cream. (pause) I think we solved the mystery of the heart attack.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh. (pause) Did you call your parents?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, yeah. Emma's doing great.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just can't believe I'm in Rachel Green's room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What do you mean? You've been in my room before!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, sure, right! Like I've ever been in Rachel Green's room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok I gotta tell ya, it's really weird when you use my whole name.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sorry. (Rachel sits on her bed). You ok?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You had a rough day, uh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, just so weird seeing him like that, you know? I mean he is a doctor, you don't expect doctors to get sick!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But we do! (pause) It's gonna be ok, Rach!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (she's sad) Ow. I don't want him to wake up alone! I should go to the hospital!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? No, no! Hey, hey, hey look...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They gave him a lot of medication, ok? He wouldn't even know if you were there. Look, we'll go see him first thing in the morning, ok?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really, I shouldn't feel guilty?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, God! Hey, Rach, you've been an amazing daughter, ok? Right now you just need to get some rest.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, maybe you're right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (he kisses her on her forehead) Good night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait, wait, wait, wait. Would you stay here with me for a little while?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok. (She sits on the bed and Ross sits near her) Thank you for coming with me today.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, of course...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Rachel Green is very happy you're in her room!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Me too. Come here. (They hug)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I just don't want to be alone tonight.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, well, uh, I can maybe grab a sleeping bag, or...(There's one of those moments. They're staring at each other, no word uttered, and then she leans toward him in order to kiss him, but he ducks and avoids her more than once.) Oh, oh. (he then hugs her and when she tries to kiss him again, he stands up and she falls down on the bed). No, Rach! I'm sorry, I just don't think this, this, this is a good idea.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait, we won't know that until we do it, will we?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, look, uh. You are upset about your father and you're feeling vulnerable and I just don't feel it would be right, I'd feel like I'd be, you know, taking advantage of you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Taking advantage? I'm giving you the advantage, enjoy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I'm sure it would be great, but I-I think one of us has to be thinking clearly, so, I'm gonna go!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow. Ok.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'll see you in the morning (he leaves).

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mhm-mh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (outside her room, talking by himself) Haven't had sex in four months, I should get a medal for that!
Monica's apartment. Monica and Erica have just arrived.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Thanks so much for taking me to all those places. I had a great time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I'm glad. Listen, I want to apologize about Chandler, though. I just did not see this coming.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (enters the room wearing an "I love New York" t-shirt, a "Statue of Liberty" hat and carrying bags) New York is awesome!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is with you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I've been to these places before, but I've never really seen them, you know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, you miss alot, when you're moo-ing.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Thanks so much for showing me around.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh! It was our pleasure. We are so much enjoying getting to know you.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Well, if there is anything else you wanna know... (Monica and Chandler look at each other)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, uhm, okay, uhm, do you mind if we ask you some questions about the father?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Oh, sure. Yeah, well, he was my high school boyfriend. Captain of the football team, really cute and he got a scholarship and went off to college. (Monica and Chandler are smiling from ear to ear)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's great.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Yeah... it's almost definitely him. (Monica and Chandler look confused now)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How's that now?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Well, there is a chance it's another guy. I mean, I have only ever been with two guys, but they sorta overlapped.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, what does the other guy do? Does he go to college too?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: No, he's in prison. (More shocked looks from Monica and Chandler)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Was he falsely accused of something? (They look hopeful)

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: No... he killed his father with a shovel. (Monica and Chandler's jaws drop) But other than that, he's a great guy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'll bet his dad doesn't think so.
Time lapse. Chandler and Monica are in bed now

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you awake?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Of course I'm awake. Assume from now on that I'm always awake! (He turns the light on)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, we don't know that it's him. I mean, it could be the football guy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey, it's us. Of course it's the shovel-killer.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, lets say that it is him, would we not want the baby? No! Would we treat him any differently?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'd keep an eye on him! We have to find out which one the father is.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I dunno, aren't there tests for these things, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, but maybe we're just over-reacting.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pff, easy for you to say, he's a father killer. He probably loves him mommy. He's probably got a tattoo that says "mom" on his shovel-wielding arm!
Joey's apartment. He is sitting on the barcalounger holding a French study book and listening to a French learning tape.

lines.speaker: Tape
lines.text: We will now count from one to five. Un, deux, trois, quatre, cinq.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh, un, blu, bla, flu, flenk!

lines.speaker: Tape
lines.text: Good job.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank you.
Phoebe enters

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Listen, I feel really badly about yesterday and I thought about it a lot and, and I know, I was too impatient. SO lets try it again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, no, that's okay, I don't need your help. I worked on it myself and I gotta say, I am pretty good!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really, can I hear some of it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure, sure. Ok, (clears his throat and starts to read from his script. He starts talking in a fake French accent, making gestures with his hands) "Bleu de la bleu, de la blu bla bleu" (Phoebe looks astonished, annoyed and disgusted, Joey seems very proud though) See?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, you're not, (she tries to smile and contain her anger, but loses it) You're not... you're not... again, you're not SPEAKING FRENCH!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (offended) Oh well I think I am, yeah and I think I'm definitely gonna get the part.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How could you possibly think that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: For one thing, the guy on the tape said I was doing a good job!
The hospital. Rachel is pouring her self a cup of coffee. Ross approaches from behind.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Rach, can you grab me a cup of coffee?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (She turns around very slowly, looks at him for a second and then turns back to her coffee) Sure. (She gives him the cup she was pouring for herself without looking at him)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You've been quiet all morning. Is everything okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hmm-hmm. (starts to pour herself a cup of coffee, never looking at Ross)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You sure you're alright?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (coldly) Yep.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (knowing she's not alright) O-kay. Well, I'm gonna go grab us some breakfast. (He starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: FYI..

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (knew this was coming) There it is... (he comes back)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: In the future, when a girl asks for some ill-advised sympathy sex... just do it. (she smiles fakely at him)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (half amused) Wait, wait, (looks around a little) You're mad at me about last night? I was just trying to do the right thing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sarcastically) Really? Well, it seems to me if you'd done the right thing, I would not have woken up today feeling stupid and embarrassed, I would have woken up feeling comforted and satisfied!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (acknowledging the last part of her sentence) Well...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh stop that!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe this. I was just being a good guy. I treated you with respect and understanding.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sarcastic) Oh, that is so hot. She walks around him to the other side)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, I was looking out for you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, really, well Ross, you know what? I am a big girl. I don't need someone telling me what is best for me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I gotta say, I have not had sex a lot of times before, this is the worst ever.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, really, really? Well, it wasn't very good for me either. (She turns to leave and Ross over takes her and stands infront on her, his back to the row of doors leading to the hospital rooms)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey you know what? You know what? To avoid this little thing in the future, let's just say, you and me, never having sex again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's right, sex is off the table. (The door starts to open behind him and Dr. Green emerges) I am never having sex with you again. (Rachel stays quiet and after a few moments Ross realizes what has happened. He turns abruptly) Dr. Green, are you feeling better? (Rachel's dad glares at him with a deadly look)
Monica and Chandler's apartment. Monica and Erica enter, Chandler is in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! How was lunch?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: (To Chandler) We had a good time. By the way, I wanted to ask you something. It would really mean a lot to me, if the baby was a boy, that you name him after my father, Jiminy Billy Bob (Monica smiles at Chandler and his he looks shocked and scared, getting no support from his wife)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (struggling) Oh, really?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: No! (To Monica) You we're right, that was fun! I'm gonna go finish packing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: O-okay. (steps closer to Monica and speaks softly) So, is she gonna take the test?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nope, she doesn't have to, I found out who the father is.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh God. It's shovely-Joe, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (smiling) No it's not.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How do you know?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Well, it turns out that Erica didn't pay much attention in Sex Ed class, because the thing she did with that prison guy... it'd be pretty hard to make a baby that way.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh God! What was it? The thing that we hardly ever do or the thing we never do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The thing we never do.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (nods in appreciation) Shovely Joe!
The theatre where Joey is auditioning. Phoebe enters when Joey's on stage and she sits down. He hasn't seen her.

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Whenever you're ready Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right. (clears his throat) Dja bu bu Claude. Uh, c'est la pu les la lu blah bloo.

lines.speaker: Casting assistant
lines.text: I'm sorry, what's going on?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, come on! French it u-up!

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Joey, do you speak French?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Toutes la smore! Bu blu-ay bloo blah ooh! Pfoof!

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: You know what. I think this audition is over. (Joey looks disappointed, but understands.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (in a French accent) Uh, excuse me. Uh, I am Regine Philange. I was passing by when I heard this man speaking the regional dialect of my French town of Estee Lauder.

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: You really think this man is speaking French?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sa-sa-saw!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ecoutez, je vais vous dire la verite. C'est mon petit frere. Il est un peu retarde. (Translation: Listen, I will tell you the truth. He's my little bother. He's a bit retarded.)
The director looks at Joey and he nods.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Alors, si vous pouviez jouer le jeu avec lui... (Translation: So, would you please just humor him?)

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: (to Joey) Good job, little buddy. That was some really good French. But I think we're gonna go with someone else for the part.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah. All right. But my French was good?

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: It was great.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Oh-hoh! Ha-hah! See!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to the director) Merci. Au revoir. (Translation: Thanks, goodbye.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah-hah. Toute-de-le-fruit.
Joey and Rachel's apartment. Rachel is sitting at the kitchen counter and Ross enters from Rachel and Emma's room.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Emma's down for the night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So uh... I guess I wanna take off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay... Hey listen, just before you go I-I again, I just wanna say "thank you" for coming with me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no problem.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And also, you know I uh, I was thinking about what you said, you know, about the whole sex thing and... it's probably not a great idea to go down that road again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you. I'm glad you agree.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's a shame though, I mean, when we did it, it was pretty good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah... Yeah, that's true.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey uhm, do you remember that one really great time...?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, ye-ah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know it was you're uhm... birthday...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...Valentine's day...

lines.speaker: Both
lines.text: (long pause, they realize) Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I guess that's all in the past, now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hmmm-mmmm.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (after a pause) Not even one more time?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not even once.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No matter how much we want it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Even if we want it really bad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's what we decided.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhm, right!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ...It's kinda hard though!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know, when two people have a connection, you know, that's... just seems like such a... waste.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...I hate waste.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ...Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Just so you know... With us... it's never off the table. (she enters her room and closes the door.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Damn it. It's never off the table. (he leaves the apartment)
COMMERCIAL BREAK
Joey and Rachel's apartment. Phoebe and Joey are "updating" Joey's resume.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, can you really tapdance?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's off the resume. (she strikes it through with a pencil)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Archery?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Horseback riding?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Would fall off a lot.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You can drink a gallon of milk in 10 seconds?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That I can do.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on! You can drink a gallon of milk in 10 seconds?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, watch me! (he takes a full container of milk from the fridge) Okay, you time me. Ready?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ready... GO!

Joey takes the plastic container to his mouth and starts to drink. Most of the milk gushes from the bottle down his chin and over his clothes to the floor. He keeps "drinking" and all of a sudden he lifts it up and half the bottle of milk pours out in an instant. He then continues to drink the rest. He then puts the empty container down on the counter.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (checking her watch) You did it!
THE END
